LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TOM CYGNAR, CLAUDIA CYGNAR, AND SPRINGSROAD CONSULTING'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. A COMPARISON OF THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT WITH THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IN 467958 SHOWS THAT THE CURRENT ALLEGATIONS EITHER REPEAT WHAT HAD BEEN ALLEGED IN THE PRIOR ACTION OR COULD HAVE BEEN RAISED IN THE PRIOR ACTION AND THE JUDGMENT AND RULINGS IN THE PRIOR ACTION BARS ASSERTION OF THE CURRENT ALLEGATIONS AGAINST THE DEMURRING DEFENDANTS UNDER RES JUDICATA AND COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL. MR. GIDDING IS GRANTED LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE, IF HE CAN DO SO, IN GOOD FAITH, ALLEGATIONS OF MISCONDUCT AGAINST THE DEMURRING DEFENDANTS THAT WERE NOT ALLEGED IN THE PRIOR ACTION OR COULD NOT HAVE BEEN RAISED IN THE PRIOR ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS STEVE VENTRELLO, FAITH VENTRELLO, VINTAGE WINE MARKETING AND VENTRELLO WINE MARKETING'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF JOHN GIDDING'S CLAIM IS BASED ON ALLEGED MISCONDUCT COMMITTED AGAINST A COMPANY FOR WHICH HE WAS A FORMER SHAREHOLDER AND A GUARANTOR. BECAUSE SUCH A CLAIM BELONGS TO THE COMPANY, MR. GIDDING LACKS STANDING TO SUE FOR THE SOLE CLAIM ALLEGED IN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. MR. GIDDING IS GRANTED LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THAT THE DEMURRING DEFENDANTS OR ANY OF THEM COMMITTED MISCONDUCT AGAINST HIM PERSONALLY AND HE SUFFERED DAMAGE THEREFROM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
